channel.title: Math Learning Site – Practice Questions
channel.description: Recent content in Practice Questions on Math Learning Site

item.title: Solving Quadratics by Factoring
item.description:
Practice solving quadratic equations by factoring and applying the zero product property.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve: \((x - 5)(x + 2) = 0\)

Solve: \(3x² - 12x = 0\)

item.title: Completing the Square Test
item.description:
Practice rewriting and solving quadratics by completing the square.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve: \(x² - 20x + 110 = 0\)

Convert y = x² - 4x + 7 into vertex form by completing the square.

Solve: \(x² - 8x + 7 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 10x - 24 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 6x + 5 = 0\)

If you add 9 to the left side of an equation to complete the square, what must you do to the right side to keep it balanced?

Solve: \(x² + 2x + 2 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 2x - 8 = 0\)

For 3x² + 12x, a student factors out 3 to get 3(x² + 4x). What number should they add inside the parentheses to complete the square?

Solve: \(x² + 2x - 15 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 4x - 5 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 10x + 26 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 4x = 12\)

Solve by completing the square: \(x² - 6x + 2 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 6x - 16 = 0\)

After completing the square for x² - 18x + 81, how is the trinomial rewritten?

Solve: \(x² - 2x = 3\)

Solve: \(x² - 4x - 21 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 10x - 11 = 0\)

A student adds 16 to the left of x² + 8x = 5 but forgets to add it to the right. What will their final constant on the right be mistakenly?

Solve: \(x² - 12x + 40 = 0\)

If the middle term 'b' is -8, what is the constant (b/2)² needed to complete the square?

Solve: \(x² - 8x + 20 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 8x + 12 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 18x + 90 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 14x + 50 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 16x + 60 = 0\)

To complete the square for x² + 5x, what fraction must be added?

Solve: \(x² + 2x - 5 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 6x - 27 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 12x + 11 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 10x + 20 = 0\)

To complete the square for 2x² + 8x = 10, what is the mandatory first step?

If an equation is completed to y = (x + 5)² - 2, what is the vertex of the parabola?

Solve: \(x² - 8x - 20 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 10x + 9 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 2x - 1 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 16x - 17 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 16x + 70 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 14x + 45 = 0\)

When solving x² + 6x - 7 = 0 by completing the square, what should you do with the -7 first?

Solve: \(x² - 14x - 15 = 0\)

To complete the square for x² + 10x, you take half of 10 and square it. What number must be added?

Solve: \(x² + 4x - 12 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² - 4x + 5 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 8x - 9 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² + 6x + 10 = 0\)

Which constant completes the square for x² - 14x?

Solve: \(x² + 12x - 13 = 0\)

The expression x² + 12x + 36 can be written as which perfect square binomial?

item.title: Quadratic Formula and Discriminant
item.description:
Practice using the quadratic formula and interpreting the discriminant.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

True or False: \(You can use the Quadratic Formula on the equation 5x + 2 = 0.\)

What is the value of b² if b = -6?

The 'discriminant' is the specific part of the quadratic formula found under the radical. Its formula is

Using b=2 and 4ac=12, what is the discriminant of 3x² + 2x + 1 = 0?

What is the discriminant of x² + 1 = 0? (Hint: \(a=1, b=0, c=1)\)

What is the discriminant of x² - 10x + 25 = 0?

In the equation x² - 7x + 10 = 0, what is the value of the constant 'c'?

In 3x² + 2x + 1 = 0, what is the value of 4ac?

Before using the quadratic formula, the equation must be in the form ax² + bx + c = 0. In the equation 3x² - 5 = 2x, what is the correct value of 'b' after moving terms?

A discriminant of 15 tells you the equation has

If a = 1/2, what is the value of the denominator 2a?

For the equation x² + 4x + 4 = 0, calculate b². What is the value?

If the discriminant is 0, the two solutions are actually

In the equation x² + x - 1 = 0, the value of 'b' is

For x² - 2x - 8 = 0, the discriminant is 36. What is the value of √D?

Since the discriminant is 49 (from the previous question), the roots are

If you only need to know HOW MANY solutions an equation has, which part should you calculate?

If x = (6 ± √36) / 2, what is the first step to simplify this?

If the discriminant is positive but NOT a perfect square (like 7 or 13), the solutions will be

Find the discriminant of 2x² - 5x - 3 = 0.

Which of these is the correct Quadratic Formula?

Why can the coefficient 'a' never be 0 when using the quadratic formula?

If an equation has no real roots, where does its graph sit relative to the x-axis?

If an equation is 2x² + 5x = 0, what is the value of 'c' to be used in the quadratic formula?

Any time b² is greater than 4ac, the equation will have

The Quadratic Formula is most useful when an equation

Given the discriminant is -3 (from the previous question), the equation x² + x + 1 = 0 has

For the equation x² + 4x + 4 = 0, calculate 4ac. What is the value?

In the quadratic formula, the entire numerator is divided by

The Quadratic Formula can be used to solve

The part of the formula '-b / 2a' represents what part of the parabola?

If the discriminant is a perfect square (like 25 or 49), the solutions to the equation will be

The values of a, b, and c come from which form of a quadratic equation?

If the discriminant (b² - 4ac) is exactly 0, how many unique real solutions does the equation have?

In the equation -x² + 4x - 7 = 0, what is the value of 'a'?

The quadratic formula starts with 'x = negative b...'. If your 'b' value is already -5, what should you write at the start of the formula?

Continuing from the last question, if -b = 2 and √D = 6 and 2a = 2, the two solutions are (2+6)/2 and

To solve 2x² - 3x + 1 = 0, what value should be substituted for 'a' in the denominator 2a?

If b = 1, what is b²?

What is the discriminant of x² - x = 0?

If b² = 25 and 4ac = -20, what is b² - 4ac?

Find the discriminant for x² + x + 1 = 0.

If an equation is x² - 9 = 0, what is the value of 'b' to be used in the quadratic formula?

When the discriminant is 0, the formula becomes x = -b / 2a. This is why there is only ___ solution.

When the value of b² - 4ac is negative, the square root will result in an imaginary number. This means the equation has

In the quadratic formula, the symbol '±' indicates that

In x² - 5x - 6 = 0, the term -4ac is -4(1)(-6). This simplifies to

Which discriminant value would indicate two real, irrational roots?

When calculating -4ac, if both 'a' and 'c' are negative, the result of -4ac will be

Using the results from the previous two questions (b²=16, 4ac=16), what is the discriminant of x² + 4x + 4 = 0?

item.title: Graphing Quadratics and Systems
item.description:
Practice graphing quadratics and analyzing systems that include quadratic relationships.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

A system of y = x² and a horizontal line has no solution. Where must the line be located?

What is the range of the function y = -(x-1)² + 5?

Identify the equation for y = x² shifted 2 units left and 3 units down.

On the graph of y = x², for which x-values is the graph 'falling' or decreasing?

If a line is tangent to a parabola, how many solutions does the system have?

If a system of a line and parabola has exactly one solution, the line is said to be ___ to the parabola.

What is the vertex of the function y = (x - 5)² + 10?

The graph of y = -x² is a reflection of y = x² across the

To solve the system y = x² - 4 and y = 5 graphically, you should look for the x-values where x² - 4 equals

The lowest or highest point on a parabola is specifically called the

Solving x² = 2x + 3 graphically involves finding the intersection of y = x² and

If a parabola's vertex sits exactly on the x-axis, how many real roots does it have?

If the vertex of a parabola is (3, -4) and it opens downward, how many x-intercepts must it have?

In y = a(x-h)²+k, if 'a' is doubled, what happens to the vertex?

Which function has the 'flattest' or widest curve?

The graph of y = (x - 2)² is the same as y = (2 - x)².

Step 1: \(Find the vertex. Step 2\)

The vertical line that cuts a parabola into two matching halves is called the

Compared to y = x², the graph of y = 4x² will appear

How many solutions can a system of two different parabolas have?

Where do y = x² and y = -x² intersect?

In standard form y = ax² + bx + c, which letter determines if the graph opens up or down?

Which equation represents a horizontal shift of y = x² followed by a vertical stretch?

Which graph will have a peak (maximum) rather than a valley (minimum)?

How does the graph of y = x² + 5 compare to y = x² - 5?

In the standard equation y = x² + 6x + 5, which constant represents the point where the graph crosses the vertical axis?

If the leading coefficient 'a' in y = ax² + bx + c is negative, the 'arms' of the parabola will point

Consider a parabola with a vertex at (0,5) opening upward and a line y = 2. How many times do they intersect?

Which graph is a U-shaped curve?

If (1, 5) is a point on a parabola with axis of symmetry x=3, which other point must be on the graph?

To find the y-intercept of any quadratic graph, you should set x equal to

In the function y = (x + 4)², the '+4' moves the graph toward which side of the x-axis?

As x increases from -10 to 0 for the graph y = x², the y-values

A line with a positive slope passes through the vertex of a parabola opening upward. What is the minimum number of intersections?

If |a| < 1 in the equation y = ax², the parabola is

A table shows points (-1, 1), (0, 0), and (1, 1). This most likely represents which shape?

For the graph of y = x² + 2, which of these is a possible y-value?

In the vertex form y = a(x - h)² + k, the value of 'k' tells you how far the parabola has shifted

To move the graph of y = x² three units to the right, how should the equation be modified?

When solving y = x² + 2 and y = 3x, the first step is setting them equal. This represents finding the points where

Does the system y = x² + 10 and y = x - 5 have any real intersection points?

If the vertex of a parabola is (3, -4) and it opens upward, how many x-intercepts must it have?

Which change to y = x² creates y = x² + k (where k > 0)?

In the function y = x² - 7, the vertex is located on which axis?

For the graph of y = ax² + bx + c, the vertical line of symmetry is always x =

If the x-intercepts of a parabola are at x=2 and x=8, what is the x-coordinate of the vertex?

A parabola opens upward. The y-coordinate of its vertex represents the

A system has solutions at (2, 4) and (-2, 4). This could be the intersection of y = x² and

Can a linear equation and a quadratic equation intersect 0 times?

Which point lies on the graph of y = 2x² - 1?

item.title: Properties of Radicals Practice
item.description:
Practice simplifying and working with radical expressions connected to quadratic work.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

To simplify √(9/16), you can take the square root of the numerator and denominator separately. This results in

Which of these can be added directly to 3√5 because they are 'like radicals'?

Which is larger: \(2√5 or √20?\)

Simplify √12 + √3. First turn √12 into 2√3. Now add them

Simplify √98. (Hint: \(98 = 49 • 2).\)

True or False: \(√9 + √16 is equal to √25.\)

Squaring a number and taking the square root are ___ operations.

To simplify √75, we factor 75 into 25 and 3. The square root of 25 is 5, so the answer is

If you square a square root, such as (√x)², the result is simply

True or False: \((√a + √b)² = a + b.\)

Simplify √(25x²). Assuming x is positive, the result is

In the expression √45, the number 45 is specifically called the

What is the result of √1?

Simplify √2(3 + √2). (Hint: \(Multiply √2 by 3, then √2 by √2).\)

The Product Property of Square Roots states that √(a • b) is equal to

When simplifying √20, we can write it as √(4 • 5). Since √4 is 2, the final form is

What is the value of √0?

If you have 4√x and add 1√x, how many √x do you have in total?

What is the square root of 144?

Why do we 'rationalize the denominator' for a fraction like 1/√3?

Simplify √(x³). (Hint: \(This can be written as √(x² • x)).\)

To rationalize 2/√5, you should multiply both the top and the bottom by

What is √13 • √13?

Between which two whole numbers does √30 lie?

After rationalizing 3/√2, the new numerator will be

√(x/y) is equal to

Which of these radicals does NOT need to be simplified further?

In the expression 7√2, the number 7 is called the

Simplify √48. (Hint: \(16 is a factor).\)

When multiplying √2 • √3, the result is

What is (3√2)²? (Hint: \(Square the 3 and square the √2, then multiply).\)

Simplify √x⁶ assuming x is positive. (Hint: \(What times itself equals x⁶?)\)

Which of these is the simplest form of √24?

Simplify 10√6 / 2√3. Divide the outside (10/2) and the inside (√6/√3).

In the set of real numbers, why is √-16 considered 'not a real number'?

Which radical is exactly equal to 9?

Simplify 10√7 - 4√7. Just like 10x - 4x, the answer is

To rationalize 1 / (2 + √3), we multiply by the conjugate, which is

Multiply (2√3) • (4√5). (Hint: \(Multiply the outside numbers, then the inside numbers).\)

When no number is written in the 'crook' of the radical (the index), it is understood to be

Simplify √200. (Hint: \(100 is the largest perfect square factor).\)

Step 1: \(Simplify √8 into 2√2. Step 2\)

To simplify a square root, we look for the largest factor of the number that is a

Multiply √2 • √3 • √6.

Which of the following is an irrational number?

Simplify √50 / √2 by first dividing the numbers inside the radical. This becomes

Simplify √(1/4). This is the same as √1 / √4, which equals

Does √a + √a = √(2a)?

While a square root asks 'what times itself', a cube root (∛) asks what number used as a factor ___ times equals the radicand?

Which factor of 32 should be used to simplify √32?

item.title: Quadratic Word Problems
item.description:
Practice modeling and solving word problems with quadratic equations.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

A ladder leans against a wall. The height it reaches and the distance from the wall are 'a' and 'b'. To find the ladder length 'c', we use

A store sells 100 items at $10. For every $1 increase (x), they sell 5 fewer items. The new quantity is

To find the initial height of a projectile from its equation, you should look at the value when t is

In h = -16t² + 40t + 5, the object was thrown upward with an initial velocity of

Revenue is (Price × Quantity). If price is 'p' and quantity is (50 - p), the revenue equation is

If you solve a quadratic word problem for 'time' and get t = 5 and t = -2, which answer do you keep?

If a square has side 's', the square of its diagonal 'd' is given by s² + s² = d². This simplifies to

The square of a number 'n' is equal to 10 more than 3 times the number. Which equation fits?

A rectangular yard uses a house as one side. If you have 40ft of fence and the width is 'x', the length is (40 - 2x). The Area is

A car travels North 'x' miles and East 'x+7' miles. The straight-line distance squared is 169. Equation

Length is 5 less than twice the width. If width is 'w', length is

A 5x7 garden is surrounded by a walkway of width 'x'. The total area is (5+2x)(7+2x). Why is it '2x' instead of 'x'?

If the side of a square is tripled (3s), the area becomes how many times larger?

Find two numbers whose difference is 4 and product is 45. If the smaller is 'x', the equation is

If a ball takes 3 seconds to reach its peak, how many total seconds until it hits the ground (if thrown from ground)?

A 20-inch wire is cut into two pieces, x and (20-x). If each is bent into a square, the area of the first square is

If h = -16t² + 100, what is the height after 1 second?

In h = -5t² + 20t (using meters), the '-5' is half of the acceleration of gravity on Earth. Is this true?

The sum of a number and its reciprocal is 2.5. This leads to the equation x + 1/x = 2.5. Multiplying by x gives

To find the price that generates the MOST revenue, you need to find the ___ of the revenue parabola.

A 4x6 photo has a frame of uniform width 'x'. The area of the photo AND frame is

If 'x' is the first of two consecutive odd integers, how do you represent the second one?

In the motion formula h = -16t² + vt + s, what does the 's' represent?

A path is h = -x² + 4x + 5. The horizontal distance 'x' where the max height occurs is -b/2a. Here, x is

When a word problem asks when an object 'hits the ground,' what value should you plug in for height (h)?

When a projectile returns to the same height it started from, the displacement is

If the area of a circle is 25π, then the equation πr² = 25π allows us to cancel π and find r is

A 10x20 pool has a deck of width 'x'. The area of the deck ONLY is Total Area minus Pool Area. This is

The product of two consecutive integers is 72. Equation: \(x(x + 1) = 72. In standard form, this is\)

To find the maximum height an object reaches, you should find the y-value of the

If Profit = -x² + 10x - 16, 'breaking even' means the profit is 0. This is found by finding the

A ball is at ground level at t=0 and t=4. At what time does it reach its max height?

The sum of the squares of two consecutive integers is 25. Which equation is correct?

If an object is 'dropped' (not thrown), the initial velocity 'v' in h = -16t² + vt + s is

A ball is thrown. h(t) = -16t² + 32t. To find when the ball returns to the height it was thrown from (0), you solve for the

A group rents a bus for $400. If 5 more people join, the cost per person drops by $4. This setup is

Two pipes fill a tank. If one takes 'x' hours and the other takes 'x+1' hours, the combined rate involves which type of equation?

A rectangle has a perimeter of 20. If width is 'x', then length is (10 - x). The area equation is

In the formula h = -16t² + s, why is the '16' negative?

If a ball hits the ground at t = 2.456 seconds, 'to the nearest tenth' would be

Can the width of a physical garden ever be a negative solution from a quadratic equation?

Two numbers have a sum of 12. If one is 'x', the other is (12 - x). Their product is

The area of a triangle is 20. If the base is x and height is (x+2), the equation is 20 = 0.5(x)(x+2). What is the first step to clear the fraction?

The area of a rectangle is 50. The length is twice the width (w). What is the equation?

An object falls from 100ft. The equation is 0 = -16t² + 100. To solve for 't', the first step is to move 100 to the other side and then

A sail's height is 4ft more than its base. Area is 30. Using A = 1/2 bh, the equation is

The hypotenuse of a right triangle is 10. One leg is x, the other is x+2. Which setup is correct?

The length of a rug is 3 feet more than its width (w). Which expression represents the area?

If time 't' is in seconds and height 'h' is in feet, the velocity 'v' is measured in

A rocket's height follows h = -16t² + 64t. At t = 0, what is the height of the rocket?

item.title: Miscellaneous
item.description:
Practice a mixed set of quadratic equation questions.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve using Quadratic Formula: \(x² - 4x - 1 = 0\)

Solve: \(x² = -16\)

Find the discriminant of x² + 5x + 4 = 0.

Simplify: \(5 / √2\)

Solve: \(x² = 49\)

On a graph, the solutions to a quadratic equation are the points where the parabola crosses the

Solve: \(3x² - 12x = 0\)

A square's area is doubled when its side is increased by 2. This leads to (s+2)² = 2s². Solve for the original side s.

Simplify the radical: \(√72\)

Simplify: \(√18 + √50\)

Solve: \((x - 3)² = 16\)

Solve by completing the square: \(x² - 6x + 2 = 0\)

Solve the system: \( y = x² y = x + 2 \)

A student solves a system of a line and a parabola and finds 3 intersection points. Is this possible?

To complete the square for 2x² + 8x, what is the first step?

If b² - 4ac is negative, the equation has

If a parabola's vertex is at (3, 4) and it opens upward, how many real x-intercepts does it have?

Solve: \((x - 5)(x + 2) = 0\)

Simplify: \(√3 * √12\)

A ball is dropped from 64 feet. Its height is h = -16t² + 64. When does it hit the ground?

A quadratic equation has a discriminant of 0. How many x-intercepts does its graph have?

In the equation 3x² - 5 = 2x, what is the value of 'b' for the quadratic formula?

To complete the square for x² + 10x, what number must be added?
